description: Original tasks before rewriting based on actual data

tasks.name: Save the most recently updated 4K streaming requirements article
tasks.description: Use Help Center search and filters to locate the most recently updated article about 4K streaming requirements and add it to your saved articles list.
steps: Navigate to the Help Center homepage of the video streaming service.
Click on the main search bar at the top of the page.
Type "4K streaming requirements" into the search field.
Press Enter on your keyboard to run the search.
In the results page, click on the "Topic" filter in the sidebar and select "Streaming quality" from the list.
Open the "Sort by" dropdown and select the option labeled "Last updated (newest)".
From the sorted results, click on the first article whose title includes both "4K" and "internet speed".
On the article page, click the "Save" or bookmark icon to add this article to your "Saved articles" list.

tasks.name: Submit a support request about smart TV buffering from a highly rated article
tasks.description: Find a help article about buffering on smart TVs that has a rating of at least 4 stars, then use the link in that article to submit a streaming quality support request describing evening buffering issues.
steps: From the Help Center homepage, click the "Browse all categories" or "Categories" link.
In the categories list, click on the category labeled "Watching on TV".
On the "Watching on TV" page, select the subcategory titled "Streaming issues".
In the article list, use the rating filter to show only articles with a rating of 4 stars or higher.
From the filtered list, click on the article whose title includes the phrase "Buffering or poor video quality on smart TVs".
On the article page, scroll down and click the button labeled "Contact support about this issue".
On the contact form that appears, open the "Topic" dropdown and select "Streaming quality".
In the "Subject" field, type "Frequent buffering on my smart TV".
In the main description or "Details" text area, type a brief explanation that includes the phrase "happens every evening around 8 PM".
Click the "Submit request" button at the bottom of the form to send the support request.

tasks.name: Choose the simpler cancellation method and mark that article as helpful
tasks.description: Compare the Help Center articles for canceling your subscription on a web browser vs. on a TV app by counting steps, then mark the method with fewer steps as helpful.
steps: On the Help Center homepage, click the "Account & Billing" category link.
Within "Account & Billing", click the subcategory labeled "Canceling your subscription".
In the list of cancellation articles, click on the article whose title includes "Cancel on a web browser".
Scroll to the main instructions section labeled "Cancel using a web browser" and note how many numbered steps are listed in this section.
Use your browser's Back button or the breadcrumb navigation to return to the "Canceling your subscription" article list.
From the list, click on the article whose title includes "Cancel on your TV app".
Scroll to the main instructions section labeled "Cancel using your TV" and note how many numbered steps are listed in this section.
Determine which article (web browser or TV app) has fewer numbered steps in its main instructions.
If the currently open article is not the one with fewer steps, navigate back to the article list and open the article that has fewer steps.
On the article with fewer steps, click the "Yes" or "This article was helpful" feedback button near the bottom of the page.

tasks.name: Save a high-rated Android subtitles article and view it in Spanish
tasks.description: Filter Help Center content for Android devices, find a subtitles-related article with a rating of at least 4.5 stars, save it, and switch its language to Spanish.
steps: From the Help Center homepage, click on the top navigation item labeled "Devices".
On the devices page, click the tile or link labeled "Android phone or tablet".
In the Android help topics list, click on the topic titled "Subtitles, captions & audio".
Use the rating filter or sort control to show only articles with a rating of 4.5 stars or higher, or to sort by "Highest rated" if a minimum rating control is available.
From the filtered or sorted list, click the first article whose title includes the phrase "Change subtitles or audio language".
On the article page, click the "Save" or bookmark icon to add the article to your saved articles.
At the top of the article, click the language dropdown menu (e.g., labeled with a language code like "EN").
From the language dropdown, select "Spanish (ES)" to switch the article content to Spanish.

tasks.name: Find parental control guidance for a 13-year-old and open related PIN article
tasks.description: Use the Help Center to locate a parental controls article focused on maturity ratings suitable for a 13-year-old, save it, and then open a related article about setting a PIN.
steps: On the Help Center homepage, click the category labeled "Account & Profiles".
Within the "Account & Profiles" section, click the subcategory titled "Parental controls & PINs".
Use any age or maturity filter control (such as a dropdown or slider) to select the option that corresponds to "Teen" or "13+" content restrictions.
From the filtered list of articles, click on the article whose title includes the phrase "Set parental controls by maturity rating".
On the article page, scroll to find the section heading labeled "Profile-level controls".
In that section, click the link or button labeled "Open profile settings in a new tab" or similar, which takes you to the profile settings page.
Return to the tab or window displaying the parental controls article and click the "Save" or bookmark icon to save the article.
In the "Related articles" area, click on the article whose title includes the phrase "Set a PIN to restrict changes" to open it for later reading.

tasks.name: Use Help Center instructions to navigate to payment settings and select a billing country
tasks.description: Find the Help Center article about updating the card used for your subscription, follow its link to payment settings, and select "United States" as the billing country.
steps: Starting from the Help Center homepage, click on the "Account & Billing" category.
Within "Account & Billing", click the subcategory labeled "Payment methods & charges".
On the subcategory page, locate the internal search field (if available) and type "update card" into it.
Press Enter to filter or search within the payment-related articles.
From the results, click on the article titled "Update the card used for your subscription" or the closest matching title containing "update" and "card".
On the article page, scroll to the section heading labeled "Change your payment method online".
Within that section, click the button or link labeled "Go to payment settings".
On the payment settings page that opens, find the dropdown labeled "Billing country" and select "United States" from the list.
Click the "Back to Help Center" link or button in the header or footer to return to the Help Center.

tasks.name: Save two articles about download limits on the Standard plan
tasks.description: Search for download limits for the Standard plan, open the article about how many devices can download on that plan, then save it and one related article about download limits for other plans.
steps: On the Help Center homepage, click into the main search bar.
Type "download limits Standard plan" into the search field and press Enter.
On the results page, use the topic filter to select "Downloads" so that only download-related articles are shown.
Use any plan filter or checkbox controls to select only the "Standard" plan, if such an option is available.
From the filtered results, click the article whose title includes the phrase "How many devices can download videos on my plan?".
On this article page, locate the "Related topics" or "Related articles" sidebar or section.
In the related section, click the link titled "See download limits for other plans" or the closest title that mentions "other plans".
On the "other plans" article page, click the "Save" or bookmark icon to save this article.
Use your browser's Back button or the breadcrumb navigation to return to the first article about how many devices can download on your plan.
On the first article page, click the "Save" or bookmark icon so that both download-limit articles are saved.

tasks.name: Start a support chat for error code VC_201 with the fastest available option
tasks.description: Find the Help Center article about error code VC_201 on computers, then start a support chat (or other fastest contact option) ensuring the VC_201 code is entered in the contact form.
steps: From the Help Center homepage, click the main search bar at the top.
Type "VC_201 error" into the search field and press Enter.
On the search results page, use the device filter to select "Computer" so that only computer-related articles are shown.
From the filtered results, click on the article whose title includes the phrase "Fix error VC_201 on computers".
Scroll to the bottom of the article until the available contact options (such as Chat, Phone, or Email) are visible.

Look at the estimated response or wait times shown next to each contact option and click the "Chat" option if its estimated wait time is under 5 minutes; if not, click the option that shows the shortest estimated response time.
On the contact form or pre-chat screen that appears, locate the input field labeled "Error code".
If the "Error code" field is empty, click into the field and type "VC_201"; if the field already contains "VC_201", leave it as it is.
Click the "Start chat" or equivalent button to begin the support interaction with the selected contact option.
